title: Reis Betrayal
wikitext:
[]: Rei's Betrayal             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Feb 08, 2011 8:12 pm Okay, so in EoE we see that Rei III betrays Gendo's version of instrumentality to go off on her own. I always assumed this was because Rei III did not have the close relationship with Gendo that Rei II had. (hence why Gendo was so protective of Rei II even though she could be replaced) However, I've recently been thinking about it and am wondering exactly what Rei III had to gain from betraying Gendo. She says something along the lines of "Shinji is calling" if I remember correctly. But what caused her to do this? Rei III didn't have the relationship with Shinji that Rei II had. Is it because the memories of Rei II are playing a role? If that's the case, does that mean that Rei II would have betrayed Gendo as well? She was certainly closer to Shinji than Rei III was. Or was she just acting on her own and figured that 3I would be best for humanity?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 08, 2011 8:37 pm The three different Rei's have distinct personalities, which is theorized to be due to Rei III having a larger portion of Lilith's soul than of Rei II (which is used to support the theory of Rei I being the resident soul of Unit-00). Rei III was much more self-aware and contrary to Gendo's plan; her affection instead lay with Shinji, which may have seeded from their "reunion" at the hospital in episode 23. Essentially, through the combined experiences of Rei II, the reaffirmation of self through Rei III, and her affection for Shinji, Rei was able to betray Gendo and act on her own (which was to, ironically, let Shinji control her). A quick search bears no results on any topics deeply related to this topic, so I suppose this thread will build the precedent for future discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Tue Feb 08, 2011 8:39 pm Rei III does seem to share some memories with Rei II. If I recall correctly, Rei III sees her own tears on Gendo's broken glasses and then has a sense of deja vu, even though she knows she's never cried before.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 08, 2011 8:56 pm DIDDY wrote: Rei III does seem to share some memories with Rei II. If I recall correctly, Rei III sees her own tears on Gendo's broken glasses and then has a sense of deja vu, even though she knows she's never cried before.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Tue Feb 08, 2011 8:59 pm Rei II did cry after her little discussion with Armisael. EDIT: Nevermind, just understood what you meant. I ought to watch whatever ep. that was again, maybe my memory fails me; but I'm quite sure Rei III remembers crying, even though she knows she's never cried (as Rei III) before. That's the impression I got watching the show, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 08, 2011 9:06 pm DIDDY wrote: Rei II did cry after her little discussion with Armisael.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 08, 2011 9:27 pm I’ve always seen Rei III betraying Gendo having more to do with her forced resurrection then with anything else. Rei II new she would be replaced when she died, but it was Rei III who had to experience it. I think Gendo forcing her to come back is what really turned her against him. It’s one thing to know something will happen but quite another to actually experience it. She didn't want to be there anymore, so her forced resurrection broke the last ties she still had with Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Tue Feb 08, 2011 9:32 pm DIDDY wrote: EDIT: Nevermind, just understood what you meant.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Feb 08, 2011 9:33 pm -

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Tue Feb 08, 2011 10:03 pm There are a few reasons for this, at least based on what I've discerned: Rei III is Rei II..... but not quite. There's a lengthy article on the wiki which concludes that the resident soul of Unit 00 is "Rei 1" (based upon the scripts for the analysis of the character in episode 25). There's very little indication that "Rei 1" is the same as the Rei I which was strangled by Naoko Akagi other than the child-form is Rei 1's preferred appearance. In any event, Rei 1 is a fragment of Rei's soul. Rei III is presumably the almagamation of Rei II (who we see throughout the majority of the series) and the fragment of herself which was in Eva-00. The analysis of her psyche in episode 25 heavily implies this senario. One can also note that when Eva-00 went berserk before the series began proper, it appeared to be attacking Gendo. After the experiment, we get our earliest peek at Rei II in the timeline. It's my guess that Rei's soul was fragmented during that particular experiment (largely because I don't see any explanation how they could be able to place Rei I's soul in Eva-00), but Rei II doesn't realize what he did until after she is once again "whole". The fragment in Eva-00 probably holds no love for the Akagi women (Naoko for strangling her first body, Ritsuko for continuing her mother's actions and reminding Rei of what Naoko did to her), as Eva-00 went berserk again (with Shinji at the helm this time) and going after a dark-haired Ritsuko (perhaps knowing she dyed her hair, and seeing right through it). Ritsuko destroyed all the clones. While this isn't too obvious, but with no more clones Rei is no longer "replacable". Even if Gendo didn't really abuse that reason to control her (at least not overtly), Rei let the idea give him all the control he needed. With the clones gone, why should Rei let it control her? Gendo used Rei as a tool to meet his own ends. Rei knows full well that Gendo keeps her around only because he can use her to get Yui back. She's the keystone to his entire plan. Beyond that, Rei has no real value to him (perhaps even less value to him than Shinji). The reasons are hidden rather well, but the key points is that Rei was used by Gendo solely for the purpose of getting Yui back. Factoring some very well hidden and justified resentment, the addition of some knowledge about herself, and the removal of his only method of control? Rei had the motives and the means to derail his entire scheme. She did so.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Feb 08, 2011 10:56 pm watch episode 23' watch episode 24' watch 25 and 26 ?????????????? Completely understand Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:04 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:06 pm I don’t believe it. About all she got from Yui were her looks.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:08 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:13 pm If you put it that way, then maybe. But I still think it’s highly unlikely. I can see any reason why Yui would call to her.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:19 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:26 pm Okay, so I've seen some pretty good evidence as to why Rei would disagree with Gendo, but I'm still wondering why she felt the need to go ahead and initiate 3I while she was at it. Was Rei so inconsiderate that she didn't care about the rest of the world and only wanted to betray Gendo? Or did she feel 3I was the only way to help Shinji, who (as several others here have provided evidence for) she felt attached to?

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:32 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: too confusing/couldn't comprehend: Yui's got her own plan afterall, and it required Lilith/Third-Impact as much as everyone else's plan, it seems.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:33 pm rhfxz1s wrote: Okay, so I've seen some pretty good evidence as to why Rei would disagree with Gendo, but I'm still wondering why she felt the need to go ahead and initiate 3I while she was at it. Was Rei so inconsiderate that she didn't care about the rest of the world and only wanted to betray Gendo? Or did she feel 3I was the only way to help Shinji, who (as several others here have provided evidence for) she felt attached to?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 08, 2011 11:38 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Yui would be calling for Lilith (or Rei to the extent she became aware of her in the Eva-01 contact experiment, or even somehow knew Rei would come into existence ahead of time due to her collaboration with Fuyu who is the one that said Rei was 'the product of his despair' or... hmm, I've already lost you with all this heresy, haven't I?) because of her plan too confusing/incomprehensible: Yui's got her own plan afterall, and it required Lilith/Third-Impact as much as everyone else's plan, it seems.
